text:
is a fictional character from the Rebuild of Evangelion film series. He is the protagonist of the series and the designated pilot of Evangelion Test Type-01.

__TOC__

Personality

Shinji in Rebuild of Evangelion, while mostly the same as his anime counterpart, comes across as somewhat more angry about his situation (although he isn't outwardly angry like his manga counterpart with Sadamoto's take on the series). For instance, he calls out Misato and other members of NERV as being selfish and bemoans the fact that he's "left feeling scared" when he and Rei have to pilot during the Operation Yashima battle. Somewhat similar to his feelings in The End of Evangelion, Shinji realizes that he "has no freedom" and "has no choice but to pilot Eva" when he attempts to run away from NERV after the Fifth Angel battle. Although he decides to stay in Tokyo-3 instead of leaving like in Episode 04 of the series, Shinji can't help but feel that he's only needed for piloting Eva and that nobody truly cares for him.

These feelings are dissolved to an extent when Misato shows Shinji the Angel identified as Lilith in Terminal Dogma, explaining to him why he and the rest of NERV have to do what they can to prevent Third Impact from happening. He agrees to pilot Evangelion Unit-01 once more, and together with Rei Ayanami in Evangelion Unit-00, the Sixth Angel is eventually defeated during the Operation Yashima battle.

Shinji's relationships with the other characters in Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone are similar to those in the series. A difference with his relationships towards Misato Katsuragi and Rei Ayanami, however, is that he is somewhat physically close to them as the film progresses. When Misato shows Shinji Lilith, Shinji lets Misato hold his hand. Also, when Shinji checks on Rei in her entry plug after the Sixth Angel's defeat, the two hold hands as Rei smiles at him.

History

Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone

Some time after receiving documents from NERV summoning him for some unspecified purpose, Shinji attempts to use a payphone to make contact when he notices the line is down. While the reason is unclear at first, it quickly becomes apparent as the gigantic and monstrous Fourth Angel slowly walks past him downing an attack copter into the street. Suddenly, Shinji is rescued from the dangerous situation by his NERV liaison, Misato Katsuragi, and brought to NERV Headquarters.

After some difficulties with navigating the maze-like compound, the two are found by Dr. Ritsuko Akagi and are brought to the hanger bay where Evangelion Unit-01 is revealed. Taken aback by the demonic appearance of the unit, Shinji is then instructed by his estranged father, Gendo Ikari, to get into the unit and fight off the Angel. Out of a mix of fear and anger, Shinji initially refuses to do as his father says and a replacement pilot is called for. After seeing the dire state of his replacement and getting her blood on his hands, Shinji ultimately changes his mind and boards the Evangelion Unit.

After launching, Shinji had no issues moving the Evangelion, however moving quickly proved to be an issue as he fell over before even reaching the angel leading to the unit sustaining a large amount of damage, at first to the arm stunning Shinji from pain before taking a several cranial impacts, knocking him out cold.

Waking up in the NERV hospital ward, Shinji has almost no memory of the events which transpired during his time in Unit-01. Mostly recovered and with nowhere to go, Shinji is assigned to his own room. Upon hearing of this, Misato insists he come live with her instead and while he initially declines the invitation, her overwhelming persistence ultimately lead to his reassignment with her taking on the role of his parental figure as needed.

After moving in and learning the difficulties of living with Misato, Shinji's life became significantly less eventful as he became a full time student during the day periodically training to pilot the Evangelion using a simulator. While he willingly trained, instead of engaging with the instruction, he instead mindlessly followed the instructions given just doing as he was told. During this time, Shinji's role as an Evangelion pilot was made public (by him) causing him to become a target of bullying by Tōji Suzuhara since his sister had been severely injured during the battle against the angel. While not bullied by him, Toji's close friend Kensuke Aida was present and showed little sympathy. In a bold defense of his actions, Shinji stated he didn't want to be a pilot, a statement which only served to anger Toji further.

After their altercation, Shinji spend his time at school alone listening to his SDAT audio player. While resting alone on the school roof, Shinji was approached by the now mostly recovered backup pilot, Rei Ayanami, stating she had received an emergency call and they were needed at NERV Headquarters. It quickly became apparent that the Fifth Angel was approaching the city and Shinji would need to pilot the Evangelion once again. As he sat in the plug waiting to be deployed, he questioned why he was piloting the Evangelion at all.

As the angel approached, the Evangelion was deployed and Shinji was told to follow his training. Unfortunately, due to his unwillingness to engage with his training, it didn't prove to be effective causing Shinji to act out of fear, spraying a minigun and masking the angel in the cloud of smoke. After running out of bullets, the concealed angel went on the offensive, managing to sever the unit's umbilical cable and throw it into a nearby mountain. While downed, Shinji spotted Toji and Kensuke nearby, preventing him from fighting out of fear of injuring them. Due to the extremely unfavorable circumstances, an order was given to bring the two into the plug, then retreat.

While the first order was followed and the two were brought into the plug, the second was not. After securing the two, Shinji deployed the progressive knife and charged the angel, stabbing its core. As the operational time depleted, the angel froze in place, ultimately being defeated. While Toji had previously disliked Shinji, after seeing him crying after the battle and the extreme amounts of stress the fight put him through, Toji realized his anger towards Shinji was misplaced.

Defeating the Fifth Angel served to improve his relationship with Toji, however it strained his relationship with Misato who had issued the ignored order for him to retreat. Shinji rationalized that he had won and that if she asks him to fight, he'll fight so there shouldn't be an issue. Angered by his attitude, Misato grabbed him and nearly struck him before ordering him to go home and rest. Once again disobeying orders, Shinji instead chose to sleep on the street and wander around the outskirts of the city, observing the damage caused by his fight. Reaching a dead end, Shinji declared he could be taken back and floodlights ignited around him as a sign that he had been followed the entire time.

Upon his return, Shinji reconciles with Misato and begins his friendship with Toji and Kensuke, coming to terms with his new life in Tokyo-3 as an Evangelion pilot.

Having worked with his fellow pilot Rei Ayanami for some time yet knowing nothing about her, Shinji became curious, often staring at her during school much to the amusement of his friends. While eating dinner with Misato and Ritsuko, Rei was brought up and Shinji was asked to deliver her new NERV ID. Arriving at her place, Shinji tried knocking on the door, however there was no response. Seeing her mailbox was overfilled, he checked the door and after seeing it was unlocked, he cautiously entered her room. Looking around the room and its disarray, Shinji's attention was drawn to a damaged pair of his fathers glasses. As he tried them on, Rei entered the room and immediately pulled the glasses away from him, got dressed and left the room. Paralized by her attire (or lack thereof), Shinji completely forgot to give her the new card. Realizing his mistake, he awkwardly followed Rei to NERV HQ, giving the card to her after her old card failed to open the door.

Now traveling together, Shinji apologized for walking into her room without permission and asked if she felt fear from piloting the Evangelion as a result of her previous injury, to which she responded that she did not, asking Shinji if he had faith in his father and his work. Due to his strained relationship with his father, Shinji abrasively stated that he did not. Disturbed by his lack of faith, Rei proceeds to slap him across the face, ending their conversation.

Out of nowhere, an Angel unlike any of the past began its siege of Tokyo-3. In order to combat against the Sixth Angel, Shinji in Unit-01 was once again deployed only unlike the other angels where standard tactics worked, as soon as Unit-01 reached the surface it was immediately bombarded by a massive beam attack damaging much of the Evangelions chest armor and knocking out Shinji before he had a chance to act.

Operation Yashima

Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance

Paragraphs Needed
Meeting Asuka and Mari
Aquarium
Eighth Angel
Nineth Angel
Leaving NERV
Returning to Pilot Unit-01 and Pseudo Evolution

As the story advances into Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance, Shinji is somewhat more accustomed to living in Tokyo-3. His friendship with Toji and Kensuke is a solid one (due to them becoming friends in the first film) and he has occasional conversations (as well as smiling around) Rei. Shinji also becomes friends (and additionally roommates) with Asuka, but so far shows no romantic interest in either pilot. Shinji briefly meets Mari Illustrious Makinami twice during the film, but he knows nothing of her, save for a few verbal exchanges. During lunch with his friends at a marine preservation institute, Shinji mentions that he learned to cook meals because Misato didn't know how to. He makes lunches for Misato, Asuka, as well as Rei during the film. Shinji hopes to get along with his father and is happy when Gendo complements him after the Eighth Angel attack. Shinji is unaware that Rei tried to surprise him by inviting Gendo to a dinner party. The plans for the party were cut short, however, by the Ninth Angel's attack.

After Shinji's inability to fight the Ninth Angel-possessed Unit-03 which carried Asuka, Gendo orders the activation of the Dummy System, which tears the Eva to pieces, unintentionally injures Asuka and enrages Shinji. He attempts to destroy NERV headquarters, but Gendo orders to cut him off from the EVA, causing Shinji to faint. Shinji has a flashback of his younger self. When he awakes, he is brought to Gendo, telling him he'll never pilot again, and leaves NERV. Misato attempts to comfort him but fails. Gendo orders the erasure of his records, intending to replace him with the Dummy System. During the Tenth Angel's atack, Shinji unexpectedly encounters Mari, who tries to convince him to pilot. Much determined, Shinji urges Gendo to let him pilot again, and he attempts to rescue Rei, who has been absorbed by the Angel. Shinji thanks Rei for her efforts of bringing him and his father together. Rei apologizes that she couldn't do anything, but Shinji says it's okay. Shinji's decision to save Rei unintentionally awakens Unit-01's pseudo-evolution and causes Near Third Impact, only to be halted by Kaworu's intervention, spearing Unit-01's core.

Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo

In Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo, fourteen years have passed since Near Third Impact. Shinji has spent that time inside the core of Unit-01, which was sealed inside a Tesseract orbiting Earth. Shortly before Unit-01's rescue operation, Asuka and Mari discuss Shinji's fate. Asuka retains feelings for Shinji and expresses this by reciting poetry to herself, From the Manyoshu, about a spouse returning home. Specifically the Somonka, the "song of love between love and women." Asuka is looking at the Moon, and the Manyoshu states that: "looking at the moon always indicates the missing feeling/lovesick." Mari also sings a song called Youngsters, about a lost loved one to Asuka. These are the remaining lyrics:

That man of yours is no longer with us.

So why are you looking for him?'You're going without a clue but is skeptical of being able to rescue him, which is encouraged by Mari, The book Mari is holding has, in its introduction, an anecdote about the phrase "The moon is beautiful":

Natsume Soseki tells students to translate 'I love you' in English class. The students thought of translations such as "I love you" (我、汝を愛su) or "I seem to love you" (僕ha、そなたを、愛Shou思U). "You guys, are you still Japanese?"

Soseki continued to talk to each other. "Japanese don't speak such suggestive wording. It should be translated as 'The moon is very beautiful'". Indeed, when men and women in the Meiji period met in public, saying "the moon is very beautiful" would mean "I love you". After this, in Japanese, the phrase, 月が綺麗ですね, "the moon is beautiful" has since been regarded as a representative romantic line in which a man and a woman confess their love in a romantic situation."Mari is prompting Asuka to express these feelings herself. whom prompts them wearing their older suits in attempt to make him more comfortable. EVANGELION 3.0 (-120 min.) prequel manga, translation at Evageeks 

After Asuka retrieves Unit-01, Shinji is salvaged from its core and is welcomed with rejection by Asuka, Misato, and the crew of Misato's ship, the AAA Wunder. Shinji is fitted with a DSS Choker to his neck that will kill him if he attempts to pilot an Evangelion again; he learns that Misato and the Wunders crew now belong to an organization called WILLE, which is devoted to destroying NERV and its EVAS. Shinji questions his rejection when Evangelion Mark.09, seemingly piloted by Rei, attacks the Wunder and retrieves him. Misato contemplates activating the choker, but holds off until Shinji is out of range.
 calling for him.]]

Shinji is taken to the ruins of NERV Headquarters. He finally meets Rei, relieved to have saved her. She takes him to Gendo, who tells him he will pilot Evangelion 13 with Kaworu. He then meets and befriends Kaworu, who fixes his broken SDAT player and teaches him to perform a piano duet. He also tries bringing books to Rei. Answering Shinji's questions about why people are hostile to him, Kaworu shows him the ruins of Tokyo-3 and explains how his awakening of Unit-01 was "the catalyst" for Near Third Impact and further ravaged the world and its remaining human population. Shinji replies he only wanted to save Rei, but Kaworu tells him humanity does not see it that way. Shinji also meets Fuyutsuki, who reveals that he didn't save Rei after all and that Rei Ayanami (tentative name), who rescued him, belongs to a series of clones of his mother, who exists within Unit-01 as its "control system." Realizing his failure, Shinji suffers from a nervous breakdown after he realizes that his efforts are totally useless. With Kaworu's encouragement, Shinji agrees to Gendo's plan of reversing the Near Third Impact by piloting Evangelion 13 with Kaworu and removing the Spears of Longinus and Cassius from Lilith's corpse inside Terminal Dogma. Before the mission, Kaworu removes the choker from Shinji and places it on himself as a sign of trust.

Shinji and Kaworu arrive in Terminal Dogma, escorted by Rei Ayanami (tentative name) inside Mark.09. Kaworu expresses reluctance to remove the Spears after noticing they are an unusual shape, but Shinji is seemingly determined to continue. They are attacked by Asuka and Mari, who have followed them into Terminal Dogma. Shinji initially refuses to attack Asuka, and says he wants to save the world by pulling the Spears, but Asuka is flabbergasted, and asks him if he is trying to start another Impact. He eventually strikes back, to Asuka's outrage. Despite their efforts and Asuka and Kaworu's pleas, Shinji uses Evangelion 13 to successfully remove the Spears. However, doing so releases the Twelfth Angel from Evangelion Mark.06, which is eaten by Evangelion 13 and triggers the EVA's awakening. Evangelion 13 flies out of Terminal Dogma and begins Fourth Impact. Witnessing Fourth Impact, Shinji starts to regret pulling out the Spears. As Evangelion 13 and Mark.09 battle with the Wunder, Kaworu tries to stop Fourth Impact by allowing the choker to detonate and kill him. The Fourth Impact is only stopped after Shinji's entry plug is forcibly ejected from Evangelion 13. Afterwards, a catatonic Shinji is found inside by Asuka, who drags him out and derides him as a "brat", criticizing Shinji for always running away from his problems. Upon meeting Rei Ayanami (tentative name), the three walk through a red desert to where Asuka says "the Lilin are."

Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time

After spending a large part of Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time in a catatonic-like state caused by Kaworu's fate, he comes back to his senses when Rei Ayanami (tentative name) shows him the affection her and his friends still have for him. After witnessing the last moments of Rei's clone and the arrival of Wunder to the village, he chooses to come back to the ship, where he's taken into custody, without a choker: Misato is sure he doesn't have any intention to break his reclusion. Before the clash in the Antartic, Asuka confess her feelings for him and he recognizes his inaction the last time they fought, gaining her recognition, however Asuka says that she has moved on from him. When the Wunder is damaged, he leaves his cell and confronts his father before his departure to the Anti-Universe, and asks Misato to allow him to pilot Unit-01 for the last time, which she accepts after taking a bullet for him and placing a choker in his neck. Once Mari takes him through the Doors of Guf and promises she'll bring him back, he reassumes control of the Unit-01 after meeting again with a long-haired Ayanami, reaches infinite syncro with the EVA and fights against Gendo and Unit-13 until discovering he can't win just by means of sheer strenght. After Gendo explains him the ultimate goal of his plan, meeting Yui for the last time, and apologizes for leaving him behind during his lifetime, Shinji receives the Spear of Gaius and reunites again with Asuka, Kaworu and Rei, speaking for the last time with each one of them and ensuring they'll return to the world. Talking to Asuka, he returns her feelings. During his reunion with Rei, he makes clear the wish he'll fulfill with the Spear: a new beginning for the world, free of EVAS, where everybody can live. Before he can use the Spear to destroy Unit-01, he is released by his mother, who alongside his father takes the Spear and destroy all the Evangelions, returning the souls of the dead back to the world. Mari finds him stranded on a beach and after leaving Unit-08 to its fate, takes him back to the rebuilt world, where they appear as adults in a train station, in a city similar to Ube, Yamaguchi. Mari walks up Shinji and approaches his neck facing his front and smells his neck: "The smell of an adult". Shinji initially looks annoyed but replies confidently, to Mari's surprise, and she removes his DSS Choker. It seems unlikely she'd have spent much time with him already or know him well if she was not used to him acting confidently She offers her hand and says, "Well then, lets go, Shinji-kun." and Shinji agrees, leaving the main gate and guiding him to the new world as they let go of each other's hands. "It was a heavy burden for me, wondering if the fans would scold me for having such a newcomer hold hands with Shinji at the end (laughs). However, there have been many ways to portray "Eva" in the past, and if you can think of this as one of the possible endings, I'll be saved."" I feel that Mari just really likes people. She likes Shinji, but I think Asuka is special to Mari. Even though Asuka treats her coldly, Mari always calls her "Princess" and respects and protects her as a special person. In addition to the battles, they have together, the scene where she cuts her hair is very quiet and calm, and it has a nice sentimentality to it. I thought the contrast between the two was well-drawn. For the scene with Asuka, it was great to be able to record her voice with Yuko Miyamura. " - Maaya Sakamoto, 3.0+1.0 pamphlet interview '-Ms. Ogata, What kind of woman was Mari Illustrious?

Ogata: I didn't know until the end ... because Shinji didn't even know her name until the end.

-It's always someone else.

Ogata: That's right, isn't it. Maybe. She was like a friend of his father and mother, right? At the end, I thought she would come to him to pick up that position. - Ogata's Newtype interview on 3.0+1.0 Sound director in a VTuber interview: In a recent interview with V-tuber Vivian, the sound director for Evangelion 3.0+1.0 says he did not instruct the seiyuus of Shinji and Mari to act as lovers, he interpreted their relationship to be that of friends. It is mentioned that the station scene was not instructed as romantic. Whether they're lovers, friends, or family is left up to the viewer. Even the seiyuu will have different opinions about it. I'm sure this is what Anno is intending, for Eva to be like a "mirror" and give back what the viewer sees in it. That is Eva's appeal. 

In Other Media

Super Robot Wars

In ', Evangelion Unit-01 goes berserk after fighting the Fourth Angel and fight Kouji Kabuto and Mazinger Z. After regaining his sanity, he and Rei rejoin the battle against the Angels.

Shinkansen Henkei Robo Shinkalion THE ANIMATION

Shinji appears in episode 31 of kids train mecha TV anime Shinkansen Henkei Robo Shinkalion THE ANIMATION. In this series, Shinji pilots the Shinkalion 500 TYPE EVA. This is a tribute to the real 500 TYPE EVA bullet train that ran on the line from November 2015 to May 2018. The 500 TYPE EVA mecha differs from Evangelion Unit-01 in that it's an actual transforming giant robot instead of an ADAM-derived cybernetic organic lifeform. Megumi Ogata reprises her role as Shinji in the episode. https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/interest/2018-07-02/kids-anime-shinkalion-goes-full-eva-megumi-ogata-reprises-shinji-role/.133698 https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/interest/2015-10-23/reports-on-upcoming-eva-train-surface-on-twitter-video-shows-off-interior/.94390 https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/interest/2018-02-05/evangelion-bullet-train-goes-out-with-an-anime-bang/.127299 Shinji would make a reappearance in the winter 2019 Shinkalion film Shinkansen Henkei Robo Shinkalion the Movie: The Marvelous Fast ALFA-X That Comes From the Future, making a small appearance in the final battle.

Shinkansen Henkei Robo Shinkalion Z

Shinji would reappear in the sequel series to Shinkansen Henkei Robo Shinkalion THE ANIMATION, called Shinkansen Henkei Robo Shinkalion Z in its 21th episode, called Sortie, Shinkalion Z 500 TYPE EVA, which aired in Japan on 17 September 2021 on TV Tokyo. https://animageplus.jp/articles/detail/39187/1/1/1 Shinkalion x Evangelion Megumi Ogata Interview Ogata once again reprises her role, as do the other featured Evangelion cast members.

Trivia

It’s unknown if Shinji was in EVA-01 during the events of Transformers Mode "EVA" crossover novel.
His paraphernalia were transported to Tokyo-3 by a company named Kuroshio Logistics. Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone - Complete Records Visual Story Edition - Setting Material Edition p.329 
The doctors assigned to Shinji were named M. FUJIYOSHI and M. SAKURAI in Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone. Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone - Complete Records Visual Story Edition - Setting Material Edition p.329 
Shinji owns a RF-877 Cougar No.7 Radio in his room. Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance Panasonic x EVANGELION (Archived)
